taxonomy: Lithocardium truncatum belongs to the family of flowering plants known as the Fabaceae, which is also commonly referred to as the pea family. This family consists of a vast array of plants, including legumes, which are known for their ability to fix nitrogen from the air into the soil. Within the family Fabaceae, Lithocardium truncatum is classified as part of the subfamily Faboideae, which comprises many diverse and complex plant species. The subfamily Faboideae is further divided into various tribes, with Lithocardium truncatum likely being part of one of these tribes, although the exact classification is not specified. Further research is needed to determine the exact tribal classification of Lithocardium truncatum.
